center.name: 蒙大拿州
center.summary: 蒙大拿州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括黑嘴天鹅、庫柏鷹、金雕等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: LeConte's sparrow breeds in select areas of Canada, such as northeastern British Columbia, across Alberta, Saskatchewan and southern Manitoba as well as central Ontario and into Quebec; and as far south as northern Michigan, Montana and Minnesota

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | pages =614–615 }} There are records of vagrants along the west coast from British Columbia south and further east of its core range in Montana, Wyoming, Colorado, Kansas, Oklahoma, and Nebraska

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the United States, they breed in northeastern and central Montana, western and central North Dakota, northwest South Dakota, and in the Red River Valley of Minnesota. Sprague's pipits winter in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: During the breeding season, their range extends to the southern interior of British Columbia, as well as Wyoming, Montana, Idaho, and Utah. They are commonly found in rocky open habitats, such as exposed rock, canyons, cliffs; however, rock is not a firm requirement, as they can also be found in large eroded sandbanks. Rock wrens are rare in urban areas, instead preferring rocky areas with at least some steep features, enough shrubbery to forage in, and ample crevices for nesting. The rock wren is very tolerant of altitudes, breeding from to above sea level;{{Cite journal |last=Patten |first=Michael A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They tend to be most migratory in the north and largely to partially sedentary elsewhere. With individual exceptions, hawks of the species largely migrate out of nearly all of their range in southern Canada as well as cooler parts of the Pacific Northwest, essentially all of Montana and northern parts of surrounding states, the Dakotas (but for southern South Dakota), the northern parts of the Great Lake states, northern New York and much of New England. Despite being classed as sedentary, banding studies have revealed that many Cooper's hawks south of the typical migratory populations engage in some variety of seasonal winter movements.Goodrich, L

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: At Mount Lorette in Alberta, approximately 4,000 golden eagles may pass during the fall, the largest recorded migration of golden eagles on earth. Here the mountain ranges are relatively moderate and consistent, thus being reliable for thermals and updrafts which made long-distance migrating feasible. Birds hatched in Denali National Park in Alaska traveled from to their winter ranges in western North America. These western migrants may winter anywhere from southern Alberta and Montana to New Mexico and Arizona and from inland California to Nebraska

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the winter, trumpeter swans migrate to the southern tier of Canada, the eastern part of the northwest United States (especially to the Red Rock Lakes area of Montana), and the northern area of the Puget Sound region of Washington state; migratory populations have even been observed as far south as Pagosa Springs, Colorado
